---
title: ""
---

# Welcome to **Yong-Yeon Cho’s** Lab

We study the molecular mechanisms that regulate cellular architecture, membrane-associated signaling, and stress-responsive transcriptional programs.

## About the Lab

Our laboratory has long been engaged in research on cancer signal transduction, protein stability regulation, and regulated cell death. In recent years, our work has primarily focused on the regulation of molecular stability associated with tumorigenesis and drug resistance. We have systematically elucidated the roles of signaling networks involving RSK2, FBXW7, STAT2, ERK3, and ELK3 in cancer cell proliferation and transformation. Concurrently, we have established and advanced a novel research framework for **karyoptosis**—a form of regulated cell death—centered on nuclear envelope homeostasis and aberrant splicing of CREB3. Furthermore, we continue to expand our investigations into innate immune and inflammatory pathways such as NLRP3, cGAS/STING, and necroptosis, as well as natural product pharmacology, drug metabolism, and toxicological translation studies. This has enabled us to develop a distinctive research profile integrating fundamental mechanisms with pharmaceutical applications.

## Research Interests

-   Nuclear membrane biology
-   Stress-responsive transcription factors
-   Regulated intramembrane proteolysis
-   Structural and computational biology
-   Karyoptosis
-   Protein Stability

## News

-   Our lab website is under construction. <!-- -   New projects and publications will be updated here. -->
